customer_anxiety_profile: Kenneth is cost-conscious and skeptical of utility pricing practices — his frustration is directed at Dominion, not at solar. He's cautious about commitment ('I still don't know that I'm ready') but self-directed toward a buy-vs-lease evaluation after his rant. His anxiety is not about solar being a scam; it's about not being rushed into a decision he hasn't fully thought through.
unresolved_concerns: Buy vs. lease comparison — Kenneth explicitly said he wants to look at both options; this must be the frame for the closer's conversation
Bill photo not yet received — the follow-up sit cannot be scheduled until the bill is in hand
No locked callback time — Kenneth said 'I'll get back to you as soon as I can' with no date attached; the closer or setter must re-engage proactively
Credit score and title status not confirmed — Joe stated the 670 threshold but never collected Kenneth's answers
sensitive_topics_raised: Strong anti-utility sentiment (Dominion fuel adjustments, wind turbine cost pass-through, data center infrastructure costs) — Kenneth is emotionally activated here; the closer should align with this frustration, not defend utility practices
Heavy travel schedule and schedule chaos — Kenneth works as a nurse, travels frequently, drives his own car for local gigs; appointment timing must accommodate this
Prior Ion visit two years ago — Kenneth remembers the previous contact; the closer should acknowledge continuity without over-referencing it
exact_promises_rep_made: Joe promised to send a text from an 801 number for Kenneth to reply with a bill photo
Joe promised to schedule the next call over text to accommodate Kenneth's schedule
closer_should_know: Kenneth was driving throughout this call — the button_up requirement of 'not driving, at computer' was never set. The first re-engagement must anchor that requirement explicitly before scheduling the sit. Kenneth is back in Hampton tomorrow; that is the concrete scheduling window the setter missed. Lead with buy-vs-lease framing — that is Kenneth's self-stated decision frame, and it's the highest-value hook for getting him to show up.
